mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate declines with t-statistic less than -1.5 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales validates sustained consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a rank IC above 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observations during mid-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 215.0, inflation expectations accelerate and trigger anticipated monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month payroll employment growth expands the labor force and consumer base, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10) across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with accelerating retail sales signal synchronized strength in durable and nondurable demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to generate a positive rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production peaks and begins to decelerate, it signals supply-side bottlenecks and impending economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a hit rate below 45 percent during late-cycle phases.
